facts: Earth's vast oceans contain ~1.37 billion cubic kilometers of liquid water.
Jupiter's icy moon, Europa, is smaller than Earth's Moon, but hides a global ocean beneath its frozen crust.
This subsurface ocean is estimated to hold *more than twice* the water volume of all Earth's oceans combined! (~2.74 billion cubic kilometers).
idea.payoff: Our solar system's largest liquid water reservoir might be an alien ocean, full of potential for life beyond Earth!
idea.title: Hidden Oceans: Bigger Than Earth's! 💧🌍

scenes.visual: A beautiful shot of Earth, slowly rotating, emphasizing its blue oceans.
Earth with a visual overlay showing its total ocean volume, perhaps as a giant transparent sphere next to it, with the number clearly displayed.

scenes.text: Europa: Icy moon, hidden global ocean.
scenes.visual: Transition to Jupiter's moon, Europa, with its icy, cracked surface. A subtle X-ray vision effect reveals a deep blue layer beneath the crust, hinting at an ocean.

scenes.text: Europa's ocean: >2x Earth's water! (~2.74 Billion km³)
scenes.visual: A side-by-side comparison: Earth with its ocean volume sphere on one side, and Europa with a significantly larger ocean volume sphere on the other, clearly illustrating the 'more than twice' fact.

scenes.text: Solar System's largest liquid water reservoir. Potential for life!
scenes.visual: A breathtaking close-up of Europa's subsurface ocean, glowing mysteriously, with faint, simple microbial shapes gently drifting, hinting at extraterrestrial life.

script.thumbnail_text: Europa's Hidden Ocean: More Water Than Earth!
